lifecycle.c(3) Library Functions Manual lifecycle.c(3)

NAME

SYNOPSIS

Functions

bool lifecycle_init_once (lifecycle_t *lc)

void lifecycle_init_abort (lifecycle_t *lc)

bool lifecycle_shutdown_forever (lifecycle_t *lc)

bool lifecycle_is_dead (const lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_reset (lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_destroy_once (lifecycle_t *lc)
void lifecycle_destroy_commit (lifecycle_t *lc)

Detailed Description

TODO: Add Doxygen documentation for module lifecycle management.

Needs documentation for: Module initialization/cleanup, lifecycle state tracking

Definition in file lifecycle.c.

Function Documentation

void lifecycle_destroy_commit (lifecycle_t * lc)

Definition at line 223 of file lifecycle.c.

223                                                {
224   if (lc == NULL) {
225     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_commit: NULL lifecycle pointer");
226     return;
227   }
228   log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_commit: transitioning DESTROYING → UNINITIALIZED");
229   atomic_store_u64(&lc->state, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED);
230 }




References atomic_store_u64().


Referenced by audio_stop_thread().






bool lifecycle_destroy_once (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 180 of file lifecycle.c.


180                                              {
181   if (lc == NULL) {
182     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: NULL lifecycle pointer");
183     return false;
184   }
185 
186   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
187   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING)) {
188     // If not initialized, nothing to destroy
189     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED) {
190       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: already uninitialized, nothing to destroy");
191       return false;
192     }
193 
194     // If dead, never allow destruction (already permanently shut down)
195     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
196       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: module is dead, no destruction allowed");
197       return false;
198     }
199 
200     // If already destroying, don't duplicate work (return false, let first destroyer finish)
201     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING) {
202       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: already destroying, skipping (first destroyer has priority)");
203       return false;
204     }
205 
206     // If initializing, skip destruction (init may still be in progress)
207     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
208       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: still initializing, skipping destruction");
209       return false;
210     }
211 
212     // Unexpected state
213     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: unexpected state: %d", expected);
214     return false;
215   }
216 
217   // Winner: state is now LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING
218   // Caller must call lifecycle_destroy_commit()
219   log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: won CAS, transitioned to DESTROYING");
220   return true;
221 }






References atomic_cas_u64().


Referenced by audio_stop_thread().






bool lifecycle_init (lifecycle_t * lc, const char *
  name)


Definition at line 14 of file lifecycle.c.


14                                                        {
15   if (lc == NULL) {
16     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: NULL lifecycle pointer");
17     return false;
18   }
19   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED;
20   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED)) {
21     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s already initialized (current state: %llu)", name ? name : "<unnamed>", expected);
22     return false; // Already initialized or in INITIALIZING/DEAD state
23   }
24 
25   /* Winner: initialize sync primitive if configured */
26   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
27     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initializing mutex", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
28     mutex_init(lc->sync.mutex, name);
29   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
30     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initializing rwlock", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
31     rwlock_init(lc->sync.rwlock, name);
32   } else {
33     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initialized (no sync primitive)", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
34   }
35 
36   return true;
37 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), mutex_init(), and
    rwlock_init().

    log_search_init(), named_init(), options_state_init(),
    server_main(), stats_init(), symbol_cache_init(), and
    timer_system_init().

bool lifecycle_init_once (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 39 of file lifecycle.c.


39                                           {
40   if (lc == NULL) {
41     log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: NULL lifecycle pointer");
42     return false;
43   }
44 
45   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED;
46   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING)) {
47     // If already initialized, just return false (no work needed)
48     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED) {
49       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: already initialized");
50       return false;
51     }
52 
53     // If dead, never allow re-init
54     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
55       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: module is dead, no re-init allowed");
56       return false;
57     }
58 
59     // If initializing, don't spin (caller may be retrying or it may complete asynchronously)
60     // Just return false to indicate this thread doesn't need to do init work
61     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
62       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: already initializing, skipping (will resolve asynchronously)");
63       return false;
64     }
65 
66     // Unexpected state
67     log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: unexpected state: %d", expected);
68     return false;
69   }
70 
71   // Winner: state is now LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING
72   // Caller must call lifecycle_init_commit() or lifecycle_init_abort()
73   log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: won CAS, transitioned to INITIALIZING");
74   return true;
75 }






References atomic_cas_u64().


Referenced by asciichat_instr_coverage_enabled(),
    asciichat_instr_runtime_get(), audio_client_init(),
    log_search_init(), and splash_set_update_notification().

150     return false;

152 }

bool lifecycle_reset (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 154 of file lifecycle.c.


154                                       {
155   if (lc == NULL) {
156     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: NULL lifecycle pointer");
157     return false;
158   }
159 
160   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
161   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED)) {
162     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: not in INITIALIZED state (current: %d)", expected);
163     return false; // Not in INITIALIZED state
164   }
165 
166   /* Winner: destroy sync primitive if configured (reset keeps same primitive) */
167   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
168     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: destroying and resetting mutex");
169     mutex_destroy(lc->sync.mutex);
170   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
171     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: destroying and resetting rwlock");
172     rwlock_destroy(lc->sync.rwlock);
173   } else {
174     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: completed (no sync primitive, allows reinit)");
175   }
176 
177   return true;
178 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), and
  mutex_destroy().


Referenced by client_crypto_init().






bool lifecycle_shutdown (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 95 of file lifecycle.c.


95                                          {
96   if (lc == NULL) {
97     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: NULL lifecycle pointer");
98     return false;
99   }
100   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
101   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED)) {
102     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: not in INITIALIZED state (current: %d)", expected);
103     return false; // Not initialized or in unexpected state
104   }
105 
106   /* Winner: destroy sync primitive if configured */
107   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
108     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: destroying mutex");
109     mutex_destroy(lc->sync.mutex);
110   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
111     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: destroying rwlock");
112     rwlock_destroy(lc->sync.rwlock);
113   } else {
114     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: completed (no sync primitive)");
115   }
116 
117   return true;
118 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), and
  mutex_destroy().

bool lifecycle_shutdown_forever (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 120 of file lifecycle.c.


120                                                  {
121   if (lc == NULL) {
122     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: NULL lifecycle pointer");
123     return false;
124   }
125 
126   uint64_t current;
127   do {
128     current = atomic_load_u64(&lc->state);
129     if (current == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
130       log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: already DEAD");
131       return false;
132     }
133     if (current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
134       log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: spinning on INITIALIZING");
135     }
136   } while (current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING || !atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &current, LIFECYCLE_DEAD));
137 
138   log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: transitioned to DEAD (was in state: %d)", current);
139   return current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
140 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), and
  atomic_load_u64().
